perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, the parties shall endeavor to maintain all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement in effect.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'shall endeavor to maintain' weakens the obligation for the terms, conditions, and covenants of the original agreement to remain in full force and effect, introducing ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law - Obligation of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: While UCC 1-304 speaks to good faith, many states' common law infers into every contract a duty of good faith and fair dealing. Replacing 'shall remain' with 'shall endeavor to maintain' suggests a weaker commitment, creating ambiguity around the intent to fully honor the original agreement's terms and conditions, potentially violating this duty of good faith.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall remain' to 'shall endeavor to maintain' introduces ambiguity and weakens the original obligation. The Law Explanation suggests this creates ambiguity around honoring the original agreement's terms, potentially violating the duty of good faith. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that 'Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' The modified text weakens the commitment to the original terms, therefore it contradicts the obligation of good faith, creating a legal contradiction as defined in the Ambiguity Legal Contradiction definition.
